QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_80

question.text: Deepa joined NPS at age 67 under the All Citizens Model. What is the maximum equity allocation under Active Choice?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Subscribers joining after age 65 (before 70) have a maximum equity allocation of 50% under Active Choice and 15% under Auto Choice. **The Trap:** The regular Active Choice max is 75%, but late joiners (65-70) are restricted to 50%. This is a protective measure for near-retirement investors. **Cross-Exam Context:** Late joiners (65-70): max equity 50% (Active), 15% (Auto), must stay 3 years minimum, same 40/60 exit norms after 3 years. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Counsel late-joining clients about the lower equity caps. Even with Active Choice, they cannot match younger subscribers' equity exposure.
